title: Jira and Confluence API tokens should not be disclosed
description: Jira and Confluence are Atlassian collaboration tools widely used for project management and documentation. Personal API tokens authenticate REST API requests to Jira and Confluence Cloud. Exposing these tokens allows access to project data, issues, documents, and user information under the identity of the token owner.
recommendation: Remove Jira/Confluence API tokens from source code. Use environment variables to store tokens. Revoke exposed tokens immediately at id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens.
impact: Unauthorized access to Jira projects, issues, sprints, Confluence pages, user data, and ability to create, modify, or delete project management and documentation assets.

checks.message: Jira Cloud API token detected (ATATT3xFfGF0 prefix). Jira API tokens authenticate REST API requests to Jira Cloud under the identity of the token owner. Exposing this token allows reading and modifying all Jira projects, issues, sprints, and boards accessible to the user, as well as accessing sensitive project management data, comments, and attachments. Revoke immediately at id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens.

Confluence Cloud API token detected (ATCTT3xFfGF0 prefix). Confluence API tokens authenticate requests to Confluence Cloud for reading and writing pages, spaces, and attachments. Exposing this token allows unauthorized access to all documentation, internal wikis, technical specs, and confidential documents accessible to the token owner. Revoke immediately at id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens.

Jira or Confluence API token detected in variable assignment. Atlassian API tokens provide authenticated access to Jira and Confluence Cloud REST APIs under the identity of the creating user. Exposing this token enables unauthorized project management operations, data export, and documentation access. Revoke at id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens.
